title: MicroPython runtime dependencies must be declared by generate and installed by deploy with mpremote mip
symptoms: Device raises ImportError: no module named 'unittest'
Generated device tests pass static subset checks but fail on real MicroPython firmware
The generated project imports a micropython-lib package but no deploy-time installation plan exists
The agent vendors library source into firmware/lib without a version, source, or license record
download_drivers.py reports DRIVER_SOURCE_UNSUPPORTED or NO_DRIVER_FILES for driver.source=micropython_lib
Generated business code guesses MicroPython-lib package APIs because the source is not copied into firmware/lib
A uPyPi driver package installs .py files during generate but loses non-Python assets such as bma423conf.bin
wrong_pattern: Assuming every MicroPython documented, micropython-lib, or uPyPi module is already present in the target firmware, or silently crawling and copying library source/assets into the project during generate.
correct_pattern: Use route A for device tests and package drivers: generate must declare runtime_dependencies.mip and deploy must install missing packages with mpremote mip install before running device tests or generated firmware. Generate should not crawl micropython-lib source into the project by default; uPyPi source downloads are only supplemental evidence/source records and must not replace deploy-time mip installation.

generate_role: Declare runtime_dependencies.mip entries with package, reason, target, verify_import, and install_phase=deploy.
Record official docs and package index evidence.
Do not install packages on the device.
Do not vendor micropython-lib source by default.
Treat driver.source=micropython_lib as a runtime-only mip dependency, not as a source-download driver and not as none/manual placeholder.
Treat driver.source=upypi as a deploy-time mip dependency using the uPyPi package URL, even when .py files are also downloaded as generation evidence.
Record uPyPi non-Python package assets in asset_files and rely on deploy mpremote mip install to install them.
Still require api_ref, README, examples, docs, or package metadata before generating calls into that package.
deploy_role: Probe the selected device for required imports.
Run mpremote mip install for missing runtime_dependencies.mip packages.
Verify target filesystem folders with mpremote fs ls, for example :lib and :lib/unittest.
Persist mip_install_result.json and feed it into deploy_result.
Classify dependency install failures separately from application runtime failures.
exception_paths: If offline deployment is explicitly requested, generate may create a vendored dependency plan with pinned source, license, and commit.
If a dependency is too large or must be frozen, route it to the firmware build or flash-firmware phase rather than normal deploy.
If target firmware lacks enough filesystem space, deploy must stop with action_required instead of hiding the failure.

fix_guidance: If device/tests import unittest, keep unittest but add runtime_dependencies.mip for package unittest.
If devices[].driver.source is micropython_lib, emit runtime_dependencies.mip with install_phase=deploy and suppress NO_DRIVER_FILES because no firmware/lib source file is expected.
If devices[].driver.source is upypi, emit runtime_dependencies.mip with package=https://upypi.net/pkgs/<name>/<version>, install_phase=deploy, verify_import, and source=upypi.
If a uPyPi package contains non-Python assets such as bma423conf.bin, keep those filenames in asset_files and do not try to represent binary content as UTF-8 file records.
If the same driver lacks api_ref/readme/examples/docs/package metadata, stop generation with a partial result and request upstream API evidence instead of copying source or guessing.
Do not copy micropython-lib source into firmware/lib unless the user chooses offline vendoring.
Ensure phase_complete exposes runtime_dependencies so upy-deploy-plugin can run mpremote mip install.
Add a deploy-time import probe result to error_context when dependency installation fails.
When mpremote mip install fails because network/proxy/VPN is unavailable, classify it as runtime_dependency_install_network_unavailable instead of a generated-code failure.
After mip install, require fs_verify records from mpremote fs ls for target package folders.
Document package source as micropython-lib or explicit GitHub package URL.
